title: Binary search - halve it every time
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: Binary search finds a value in a sorted array by checking the middle and throwing away the half that can't contain it - so it needs only about log n steps.
explanation: If the array is sorted, you don't have to look at every item. Check the middle: if it's your target, done; if your target is bigger, the answer is in the right half, so ignore the left; smaller, ignore the right. Each step halves what's left, which is why a million items take only about twenty checks. The catch is in the first word - the array must be sorted, or none of that reasoning holds.

exercises.question: What does this print? (it returns the index)
hints: It returns the position of 7, not 7 itself.

exercises.question: Binary search only works if the array is...
hints: The 'throw away half' logic depends on order.
Without it, smaller/bigger tells you nothing.
